item.title: Mutares Is Said to Mull Sale or IPO of Portuguese Firm Efacec
item.description: Mutares SE & Co. is exploring options for Portuguese engineering group Efacec, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: BioNTech Investor Förtsch Says Founders’ Spinout Raises Concerns
item.description: German media mogul and investor Bernd Förtsch is calling on BioNTech SE to provide more details on the planned departure of its founders and the transfer of assets to their new venture.

item.title: Oil Industry Makes Tentative Moves Ahead of Deal to Open Hormuz
item.description: The oil and shipping industry remains largely in wait-and-see mode ahead of Friday’s deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, although there are some tentative signs of preparations as a handful of ships begin rerouting toward the Middle East while Iranian tankers laden with oil move out.

item.title: VSCO Challenges Adobe With New App and a $500 Plan for Pros
item.description: VSCO, the photography platform best known for its popular image filters, is introducing a more advanced iOS app and an all-in-one subscription plan, moves that put it in direct competition with Adobe Inc. and other brands offering professional-grade creative software.

item.title: US Order on Anthropic Models Signals New Era for AI Controls
item.description: The confusing matter of Mythos.

item.title: ASML CEO Warns of Possible Supply Constraints on Musk’s Terafab
item.description: ASML Holding NV Chief Executive Officer Christophe Fouquet said the chip equipment maker has to make sure it doesn’t experience supply constraints when servicing new projects such as Elon Musk’s Terafab.

item.title: Billions in SpaceX Trades Jolt ETFs Including Cathie Wood’s ARKK
item.description: A series of unusual multibillion-dollar flows has rippled through the exchange-traded fund market in the past week, suggesting some major investors used a contentious method to get exposure to the initial public offering of SpaceX. At least one fund manager placed temporary restrictions on its product in a bid to curb the practice.

item.title: Bulgaria to Oppose EU Sanctions Against Russian Patriarch Kirill
item.description: Bulgaria’s new government will oppose sanctions against the head of the Russian Orthodox Church included in the latest European Union package of measures targeting Moscow, the country’s foreign minister said.

item.title: Citadel Securities Saw ‘Astronomical’ Retail Trading in SpaceX
item.description: Retail investors poured into US stocks at a record pace on the day of SpaceX’s blockbuster initial public offering and are increasingly driving the broader rally in equities, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: BofA Public Finance Head Sees AI Boosting Underwriting Dominance
item.description: Matthew McQueen, who oversees Bank of America Corp.’s public finance department, is looking to artificial intelligence to expand the bank’s underwriting business across the country.

item.title: BYD Takes On Luxury EV Rivals in China With More Affordable SUV
item.description: BYD Co. showed off its largest electric car to date under its namesake brand, as the Chinese automaker attempts to challenge premium rivals with a more affordable sport utility vehicle.

item.title: Iraq Takes Steps to Boost Oil Exports When Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Iraq is preparing to boost oil exports from its southern ports with a formal deal to open the Strait of Hormuz scheduled to be signed on Friday.

item.title: US Shale Stays in the Money Despite Oil Price Drop
item.description: Long-dated prices still support drilling as buyers seek alternatives to Middle East barrels.

item.title: SEC Proposal to Scrap Decades-Old Stock Rule Has a Clear Winner: Crypto
item.description: A proposal by the top US securities regulator to eliminate a rule that mandates stock investors get the best possible prices for their transactions will benefit a new and growing corner of the financial market: crypto.

item.title: CarMax Falls as Turnaround Fails to Stem Used Vehicle Slide
item.description: CarMax Inc.’s shares dropped after same-store sales and used-car profit declined for a fourth straight quarter, raising questions about the durability of its turnaround effort.

item.title: The Latest on Allbirds’ Abrupt Pivot to AI: a New Name and CEO
item.description: Allbirds Inc., the maker of once-viral wool sneakers that suddenly announced a pivot to artificial intelligence earlier this year, has officially renamed itself Smartbird Inc. and is tapping the former leader of a Danish AI infrastructure group as its new chief.

item.title: Australia Probes Private Credit Lenders, Warns on Valuations
item.description: Australia’s corporate watchdog is investigating a number of private credit funds and warning industry managers to make sure that asset valuations are “grounded in realistic assumptions” before an end-of-month reporting deadline.

item.title: US Pending Home Sales See Biggest Gain Since September 2024
item.description: Pending sales of US existing homes had their strongest gain in nearly two years in May, sustaining a slight rally in the beleaguered home resale market.

item.title: ‘Summer of the Bond Market’ Is Teed Up for Warsh, Goncalves Says
item.description: As new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh leads his first policy meeting, falling oil prices are set to smooth the way for a rally in fixed income, according to MUFG’s chief of US macro strategy, George Goncalves.

item.title: Botswana Eyes Power Exports With $100-Million Solar Plant
item.description: Botswana is set to begin construction of its first large-scale solar plant after developers secured $100 million in financing, as investors bet that rising electricity demand across southern Africa will create a lucrative market for surplus power.

item.title: Wrong-Way AI Trade Costs Florida Stock-Picker $50 Billion
item.description: It was 180 degrees off the hot trade on Wall Street.

item.title: India’s BSE to Unveil New Index to Boost Ethical Investing
item.description: BSE Index Services Pvt. has introduced a measure of Indian stocks that are aligned with so-called “saatvik” principles in an effort to offer investors a choice based on ethical and cultural practices.

item.title: Google’s Gemini-Powered AI Home Speaker Goes on Sale June 25
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google said that its Google Home Speaker, a $100 device that includes deep integration with the company’s Gemini artificial intelligence platform, will be available starting June 25.

item.title: World Cup Travel Is Up for All US Host Cities Except for One
item.description: Trump’s bickering with Canada and geographic isolation are working against Seattle

item.title: Haaland’s World Cup Debut Draws Sponsors Betting on US Stardom
item.description: Last week, Erling Haaland sat with fans and watched the NHL’s Carolina Hurricanes close in on the Stanley Cup. One of the world’s best football players is unlikely to get away with such anonymity for much longer.

item.title: Warsh Has Potential to Spur Market Volatility, Fidelity Says
item.description: Investors from Fidelity say new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh stands to stoke bond-market volatility by offering his views on inflation.

item.title: The Trump Phone Is More Novelty Than Serious Smartphone Contender
item.description: The Trump phone is finally here — sort of — and it bears little resemblance to the original pitch.

item.title: US Retail Sales Rise in Broad Gain Despite Higher Gas Prices
item.description: US consumers ramped up spending in May across a broad range of retailers, signaling households powered ahead despite higher gasoline prices.

item.title: ECB’s Lagarde Warns AI Is Huge Risk for Financial Stability
item.description: Artificial intelligence can potentially lead to dangerous financial crises, and the European Central Bank is determined to ensure that doesn’t happen, President Christine Lagarde said.

item.title: Macron Seeks Way Around Trump’s Ban on Anthropic’s AI Models
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron will lead talks with Group of Seven leaders and tech executives to explore how to deploy the most advanced artificial intelligence models through so-called trusted partners, after the US blocked foreign nationals from accessing the technology.
